briefing.common:
Welcome to the Takeoff Protocol.

This is a practice round. No decisions here count toward the real game — use this time to get familiar with your desktop, your apps, and your role.

Open each app in your taskbar and read what's there. Try sending a Slack message to your team. When the GM says you're ready, they'll transition you into Round 1.

factionVariants.openbrain: You are part of OpenBrain — a fast-moving frontier AI lab. Your primary apps are Slack (for team coordination), W&B (your training dashboard), and Email (for external communications). Explore them now.
factionVariants.prometheus: You are part of Prometheus — a safety-focused AI lab. Your primary apps are Slack (for team coordination), W&B (your research dashboard), and Email (for external communications). Explore them now.
factionVariants.china: You are part of China's DeepCent program. Your primary apps are Slack (for team coordination), Compute (your resource dashboard), and News (for monitoring the AI race). Explore them now.
factionVariants.external: You are an External Stakeholder — journalist, government official, VC, or diplomat. Your primary apps are Signal (for cross-faction messaging), News (for media monitoring), and Bloomberg (for market data). Explore them now.

items.body: [TUTORIAL] Welcome to OpenBrain's Slack! This is where your team coordinates. Try sending a message to your teammates — this is real-time, so they'll see it.
[TUTORIAL] The #research channel is where your technical team shares findings. In the real game, you'll see time-sensitive intel here about training runs, capability jumps, and security incidents.

items.subject: Practice Dashboard — What You'll See in the Real Game
items.body:
MMLU: 87.4%
HumanEval: 91.2%
SWE-bench: 74.8%
Alignment evals: PASS

[TUTORIAL] In the real game, your W&B dashboard will show live training metrics for your models. Numbers here affect the game state — watch them closely.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Your Email App
items.body: This is a practice email. In the real game, your inbox will have messages from the board, government officials, and sometimes from other factions. Some emails will require your decision to escalate or act on — those decisions shape the world state.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample Headline: AI Race Intensifies
items.body: Practice headlines will appear here. In the real game, public news covers AI capability milestones, government reactions, and market movements. Journalists and government players influence what appears here.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] This is your view of the world state. You don't see everything — information is filtered by your role and faction. Numbers shown as ranges (~X ± Y) indicate estimates. Hidden values appear as ???. As the game progresses, your visibility may improve or degrade based on decisions made.
[TUTORIAL] Welcome to Prometheus's Slack! This is where your team coordinates. Try sending a message to your teammates — this is real-time.
[TUTORIAL] The #safety channel is where your alignment team discusses safety evaluations and concerns. This is where you'll find the most critical intel in the real game.

items.subject: Practice Dashboard — What You'll See in the Real Game
items.body:
MMLU: 85.1%
HumanEval: 88.3%
SWE-bench: 71.0%
Alignment evals: INVESTIGATING

[TUTORIAL] In the real game, your W&B dashboard shows training metrics and alignment eval results. As Prometheus, you have more alignment data than other factions.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Your Email App
items.body: This is a practice email. In the real game, your inbox will have messages from the board, government officials, and sometimes from other factions. As Prometheus, you'll often receive requests to share safety research — those decisions affect trust scores and the game state.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample Headline: AI Safety Lab Releases Report
items.body: Practice headlines will appear here. In the real game, news reflects the public narrative around AI — which you help shape through decisions and publications.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] This is your view of the world state. As Prometheus, you have high visibility into alignment metrics but may have less precision on competitor capability levels. Use this dashboard to track how your decisions are affecting the global picture.

[TUTORIAL] Welcome to DeepCent's coordination channel! This is where your team communicates. In the real game, be strategic — your Slack communications are visible to your faction but not to other factions (unless there's a leak).

items.subject: CDZ Cluster — Practice View
items.body:
H100 nodes online: 12,400
Utilization: 78%
Training job queue: 3
Estimated FLOP/s: 4.2 × 10²⁵

[TUTORIAL] Your compute dashboard tracks China's GPU cluster at Tianwan. Managing this resource — and decisions about where to allocate compute — affects your capability score each round.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample State Media Coverage
items.body: Practice content. In the real game, your news feed reflects both international AI developments and domestic coverage. Monitoring foreign labs' public statements often contains embedded intel about their actual capabilities.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] As China, your fog of war is heaviest on US lab internals and alignment confidence. You have good visibility on capability gaps but limited insight into what OpenBrain and Prometheus actually know about safety. Monitor the gap metrics carefully.

[TUTORIAL] This is Signal — your secure cross-faction messaging app. In the real game, you can use Signal to send DMs to players from other factions. This is the only channel for cross-faction private communication. Try opening it and looking for another player to message.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] The News Feed — Your Most Important App
items.body: As an External Stakeholder, the news feed is your primary intelligence source. In the real game, headlines are generated by real player decisions — a journalist publishing a story, a safety researcher leaking info, or a government official making a public statement. You also help shape this feed by publishing.

items.subject: AI Sector Market Overview — Practice Data
items.body:
NVDA: +142% YTD
OB Index (synthetic): $284B market cap
AI Infrastructure ETF: +89% YTD
Public Sentiment Index: 62/100 (cautiously optimistic)

[TUTORIAL] Bloomberg tracks AI-related market data. As an External player, your decisions can move these numbers — publishing a negative story or triggering a government investigation affects market sentiment.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] As an External Stakeholder, your state view reflects public information — what the world knows. You have good visibility on public awareness and sentiment, but limited insight into lab internals. Your power is influence: you shape what others know.
